title: Show AS score for quick_peek domains in Insights portfolio table
author: davidsurosegoro
body: Peek domains store their score in `asg` field, not `latestAs`. extractAs() now falls back to `d.asg` so peek domain scores render correctly on the Insights page (same as DomainsPage).

title: Use absolute callbackURL for Google OAuth redirect (v0.14.2)
author: davidsurosegoro
body: Google OAuth was redirecting to localhost:3001 (Sutra) instead of localhost:5175 (Astra) after login because callbackURL was a relative path. Fixed to use window.location.origin + from, matching magic link.

title: Discovery, focus, VS config, domains, Quick Peek client routes (v0.8.0–v0.9.0)
author: adsvisory
body:
## Summary
- **Discovery routes:** Focus discovery trigger, latest result, apply selected focuses, prompt CRUD (create/update/toggle/delete)
- **Focus routes:** Full focus CRUD proxy, PB status/trigger/rebuild, focus count enforcement by tier
- **VS Config routes:** Platform listing, snapshot CRUD, probe analytics, fVS history, autorun toggle, platform override, resolved config
- **Domain routes:** Domain list/detail/search proxy, Quick Peek signal endpoint with usage tracking
- **Account:** Avatar upload/clear with data URI + URL support
- **Infra:** Quick Peek usage repo + migration, pipeline detail migration, tier config additions, auth hardening, language passthrough on discovery

## Known issues from review (to fix post-merge or in follow-up)
- [ ] `x-dev-tier` header override active in production — should be dev-only
- [ ] Unvalidated type assertions on some request bodies (discovery prompts, account avatar) — add Zod schemas
- [ ] Quick Peek missing domain access check — allows scanning unowned domains
- [ ] Avatar endpoint missing audit logging and inconsistent response envelope
- [ ] Empty catch blocks in focus count checks (focuses.ts)
- [ ] Query params on vs-config routes not validated

## Test plan
- [ ] Discovery: trigger run, poll latest, apply focuses
- [ ] Focus CRUD: create, update, delete, list — respect tier limits
- [ ] VS: list platforms, trigger measurement, poll snapshot, view analytics
- [ ] Quick Peek: signal scan, monthly usage counter increments
- [ ] Avatar: upload data URI, upload URL, clear — verify DB update
- [ ] Auth: all routes require valid session, tier checks enforced

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Introduces Insights Hub API and VS Features
author: adsvisory
body:
This release ships a new API contract for the Karsa Insights Hub and introduces significant client-facing features for Verisimilitude Score (VS), Discovery, and Quick Peek functionality.

### Summary of Changes

*   **Introduces Insights Hub API Contract:** Documents the new unified API for Authority Score (AS) industry benchmarks, combining measured and estimated domain data. This contract lays the foundation for future analytics and VS endpoints.
*   **Implements Verisimilitude Score (VS) Features:** Adds comprehensive client routes for configuring and accessing VS data, including AI platform management, historical snapshot viewing, detailed probe analytics, and platform override settings for fine-grained control.
*   **Adds Discovery and Focus Management:** Introduces new client endpoints to enable content discovery, manage strategic content 'focuses', trigger discovery runs, apply recommended focuses, and generate/manage prompt briefs and individual prompts.
*   **Integrates Quick Peek Functionality:** Provides a new client endpoint (`/api/client/domains/quick-peek`) to retrieve Authority Signal scores for any domain, complete with a 7-day cache and monthly usage quotas enforced per tier.
*   **Enhances User Account Management:** Enables clients to update their primary domain and user profile pictures, including validation for image inputs.
*   **Hardens Authentication:** Corrects email verification and magic link URLs to prevent hostname misconfiguration behind proxies and refines cookie handling for better compatibility with modern deployment environments.
*   **Updates Tier Configuration:** Adds new feature flags (`pipeline_detail`) and usage limits (`quick_peek_monthly`) to the tiering system, allowing granular control over access to the new functionalities.
*   **Enables Client Domain Removal:** Allows clients to remove domains from their organization through a new endpoint, ensuring proper access revocation and cache invalidation.

Relates to Creating-VS
